pois.name: Inside Passage Waterway
pois.notes: 1,000+ mile protected waterway from Seattle/Vancouver to Alaska. Sheltered by islands from Pacific swells.

pois.name: Ketchikan
pois.notes: First Alaska port of call. Totem poles, salmon, Creek Street. Most ships dock here.

pois.name: Juneau
pois.notes: Alaska state capital. Mendenhall Glacier, whale watching, helicopter tours.

pois.name: Skagway
pois.notes: Northern terminus. Gold Rush history, White Pass & Yukon Route railroad.

pois.name: Glacier Bay National Park
pois.notes: Permit-required national park. 8-10 hours scenic cruising. Rangers board the ship.

pois.name: Tracy Arm Fjord
pois.notes: Narrow fjord with Sawyer Glaciers. Icebergs and harbor seals on ice floes.

pois.name: Sitka
pois.notes: Former Russian capital of Alaska. Raptor Center, totem park, Russian heritage.

pois.name: Grenville Channel
pois.notes: Narrow 45-mile passage between mainland and islands. Forest close enough to hear birdsong.

pois.name: Lynn Canal
pois.notes: Deepest fjord in North America (2,000+ feet). Route to Skagway and Haines.

pois.name: Chatham Strait
pois.notes: Major whale watching corridor. Humpback whales concentrate here to feed on krill.
